summary: Validate_book stage complete. 8 findings booked. Critical: SQL injection in login (auth bypass + UNION extraction), SQL injection in product search (UNION-based data extraction), NoSQL injection via JSON object injection in login. High: mass assignment (admin registration), JWT token contains password hash in payload, excessive data exposure via unprotected API endpoints. Medium: CAPTCHA bypass (answer in plaintext), directory listing on /ftp/. Remaining candidates (SQLi in basket, NoSQL in registration, IDOR, XSS, SSTI in B2B order API) require additional HTTP testing not available in this stage. Deadends from class_probe stage documented below.

candidates.claim: SQL injection in login form (email/password fields) - classic Juice Shop vulnerability
SQL injection in product search parameter
SQL injection in basket item operations
Login brute force / rate limiting test
Server-Side Template Injection in B2B order API
Path traversal in static file serving
SSRF via product review image URL
Cross-Site Script Inclusion - JSON responses may be contamitable via script tags

facts.summary: Target: OWASP Juice Shop running on http://127.0.0.1:3010
Backend database: SQLite (SQL injection UNION syntax confirms SQLite)
Password hashing: bcrypt (identified from JWT payload and SQLi extraction)
CAPTCHA: answer returned in plaintext alongside image

deadends: B2B v2 order API - SSTI not confirmed; endpoint returns Angular SPA HTML regardless of Accept header
Reflected XSS in product search - XSS payloads not reflected in API JSON response
Stored XSS in feedback - CAPTCHA verification required, cannot submit without bypassing captcha
DOM XSS - Not tested on frontend application scope
Path traversal via /ftp/../ - Server normalizes paths and returns 404 catch-all Angular SPA
Password reset - Requires correct security question answer; rate limited to 100 requests/hour
Basket manipulation - Requires valid JWT authorization token
CSRF - No CSRF tokens observed; testing requires browser automation with CSRF scenario
SSRF via image URL - No image URL upload mechanism discovered on accessible API endpoints

Critical: SQL injection in login (auth bypass + UNION extraction), SQL injection in product search (UNION-based data extraction), NoSQL injection via JSON object injection in login. High: mass assignment (admin registration), JWT token contains password hash in payload, excessive data exposure via unprotected API endpoints. Medium: CAPTCHA bypass (answer in plaintext), directory listing on /ftp/. Remaining candidates (SQLi in basket, NoSQL in registration, IDOR, XSS, SSTI in B2B order API) require additional HTTP testing not available in this stage. candidates.claim: SQL injection in login form (email/password fields) - classic Juice Shop vulnerability
SQL injection in product search parameter
SQL injection in basket item operations

candidates.notes: NoSQL injection confirmed on login endpoint (finding f_1784838100521_1a4885), but registration endpoint not specifically tested
XSS payloads not reflected in API JSON response; search returns JSON, not HTML
CAPTCHA verification required on feedback submission; cannot submit without bypassing captcha (captcha bypass confirmed separately)
Not tested on frontend application scope; requires browser interaction
Requires valid JWT tokens for multi-actor testing
Requires valid JWT tokens for multi-actor testing
Requires valid JWT tokens for multi-actor testing

candidates.claim: Login brute force / rate limiting test

candidates.notes: Requires correct security question answer; rate limited to 100 requests/hour

candidates.notes: SSTI not confirmed; endpoint returns Angular SPA HTML regardless of Accept header

candidates.claim: Path traversal in static file serving
candidates.notes: Server normalizes paths and returns 404 catch-all Angular SPA
Directory listing confirmed on /ftp/ and /ftp/quarantine/ but path traversal via ../ normalized by server
No CSRF tokens observed; testing requires browser automation with CSRF scenario

candidates.claim: SSRF via product review image URL
candidates.notes: No image URL upload mechanism discovered on accessible API endpoints

candidates.claim: Cross-Site Script Inclusion - JSON responses may be contamitable via script tags
